hero.desc: Ignite creativity, spread the passion for science & technology for students of Ngoi Sao Hoang Mai School. The journey to build the future starts today.

home.step1_desc: Browse 4 STEM fields and suggested topics below.
home.step2_desc: Download application forms, PPT templates and presentation guides above.
home.step3_desc: Register your team, prepare documents and submit before the Preliminary deadline.
home.timeline_desc: Event timeline and detailed schedule of STEM Festival 2026.
home.fields_desc: Click on each field to see suggested topics and detailed requirements.
home.round1_desc: Submit ideas via PowerPoint, technical design drawings.
home.round2_desc: Display and operate physical models at the STEM booth.
home.criteria_desc: The jury evaluates based on the following criteria.
home.faq_desc: Quick answers to common questions about the competition.
home.activities_desc: Besides the main competition, the festival features 5 exciting experience stations.

Science.title: Science
Science.desc: Focus on life sciences, green energy, and applied chemistry.
ideas: Bio-product research for effective organic waste treatment.
Creating new biodegradable materials to replace plastic.
Essential oil extraction and natural soap-making projects.
Science.req: Projects must clearly demonstrate underlying scientific principles with experimental data proving environmental protection.

Technology.title: Technology
Technology.desc: Applying programming, IoT, and AI to green solutions.
ideas: IoT systems for monitoring air quality and soil moisture.
Apps/Websites to raise environmental protection awareness.
AI-powered trash cans for automatic waste sorting.
Self-driving robots for cleaning and waste collection.
Technology.req: Must include software products or electronic circuits/microcontrollers (Arduino, Microbit...) with real code applications.

Engineering.title: Engineering
Engineering.desc: Design, assemble and build a physical model.
ideas: Smart automatic irrigation system from recycled materials.
Pulley system and mechanical waste compactor for schools.
Engineering.req: Product must be a complete physical model, prioritizing 100% recycled materials for environmental protection.

Mathematics.title: Mathematics
Mathematics.desc: Using geometry, statistics, and optimization algorithms.
ideas: Mathematical model for school waste statistics reporting.
Optimal geometry design for green architecture.
Path optimization algorithm for waste-cleaning robots.
Data analysis and pollution measurement charts.
Mathematics.req: Teams must clearly demonstrate mathematical application (formulas, graphs, 3D models) in practical problems.

passport_page.title: STEM Passport
passport_page.desc: Stamp each station you visit. Complete all to earn the Golden Passport!
passport_page.gold_desc: Congratulations! You've completed all STEM stations!
passport_page.hint: 💡 Tap each station when completed. Data is saved on your device.
